mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals accelerating labor demand, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives immediate positive equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which expands corporate profit margins and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant real credit growth indicate stagflationary pressure, which erodes corporate margins and forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where CPI rises and RSAFS declines simultaneously over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP growth signal shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward equity returns (p > 0.10) across two full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate drop below 8.0% signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage growth, stimulating consumption-driven equity rallies within the next week.
failure_condition: The threshold crossing produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling consumer prices indicates supply-side efficiency gains, which expand profit margins and drive positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative information coefficient for 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, a federal funds rate increase above 0.50% signals aggressive liquidity withdrawal, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified tightening phases over a 3-year backtest.
